summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting covering personnel changes, new employee policies, infrastructure improvements, and several appointments. The board accepted three fire department resignations due to competitive full-time job offers elsewhere, adopted revised part-time PTO and exempt accrual time policies, approved resolutions for credit card payment acceptance and fund transfers for fire station construction, renewed a five-year body camera contract largely covered by a grant, approved ARPA-funded concrete and parking lot improvements, and made several appointments following an executive session.
decisions: Adopted the meeting agenda
Approved payment of bills from April 24 to May 14, 2024
Accepted resignation of part-time firefighter EMT Jacob Myrick effective May 11, 2024
Accepted resignation of part-time firefighter paramedic Rylan Childs effective May 19, 2024
Accepted resignation of full-time firefighter paramedic Tim Harrison, changing to part-time at $19.50/hour effective May 27, 2024
Adopted revisions to part-time PTO leave policy
Adopted exempt accrual time policy for salaried employees
Approved Resolution R-008-2024 authorizing acceptance of payments by financial transaction devices (credit cards)
Approved Resolution R-009-2024 for transfer of funds between funds ($2,193 into account 4901 for fire station construction)
Accepted $800 gift card donation from CPAAA for police, fire, service, and administration departments
Declared surplus Rossi .22 caliber rifle for sale on govdeals.com to licensed FFA dealer
Approved five-year Axon body camera renewal for $54,962.76
Approved Embree Construction Services for concrete replacement at admin building and PD not to exceed $6,000 from ARPA funds
Approved Feral Seal Coating for parking lot work not to exceed $6,500 from ARPA funds
Approved purchase of rubber parking blocks and fasteners from CLEAM not to exceed $1,800 from ARPA funds
Adjourned into executive session at 6:23 PM under ORC 121.22(G)(1)
Appointed John Purcell as alternate on Board of Zoning Appeals with term expiring 5/14/25
Appointed Kevin Maloff as volunteer zoning consultant effective upon acceptance of job description
Promoted Evan Cordray from intern to temporary part-time administrative assistant at $10.50/hour effective May 14 through August 24, 2024
Adjourned meeting at 9:08 PM
action_items: Fiscal officer to obtain quotes from credit card companies/banks and select a provider for payment processing
Staff to review and provide feedback on Palmarama booth trifold brochure draft
Chief Myers to finalize insurance papers for Station 18 contract with prosecutor's office
Chad Meadows to fix potholes in parking lot in-house and install hot box asphalt curbs before seal coating
Donna Rosick to process Axon body camera grant reimbursement (~$48,000)
Detective Greg McAllister to coordinate Axon body camera deployment
Install rubber parking blocks after sealing and painting is completed
Kevin Maloff to receive and accept job description for volunteer zoning consultant role
key_amounts: $54,962.76 for five-year Axon body camera renewal
~$48,000 grant from Donna Rosick to cover majority of body camera cost
$6,000 not to exceed for concrete replacement from ARPA funds
$6,500 not to exceed for parking lot seal coating from ARPA funds
$1,800 not to exceed for rubber parking blocks from ARPA funds
$2,193 transferred into fund 4901 for fire station construction
$19.50/hour part-time pay rate for Tim Harrison
$10.50/hour pay rate for Evan Cordray as temporary part-time administrative assistant
notable_topics: Police Memorial Week and remembrance of the Durbin family
Fire department staffing losses due to competitive full-time job market
Revised part-time PTO leave policy and new exempt accrual time policy
Credit card payment acceptance for township services
Fund transfer for fire station construction accounting
Surplus firearm disposal from court forfeiture
Axon body camera renewal with grant funding
ARPA-funded building and parking lot maintenance projects
Board of Zoning Appeals alternate appointment (John Purcell)
Upcoming Memorial Day ceremony and Cleanup Day June 1
Chamber dinner and police officer recognition for Officer Kyle Howell
Medic unit expected back mid-June to mid-July after liquid spring parts repair
notes: Executive session lasted approximately 2 hours and 41 minutes (6:23 PM to 9:04 PM), which is unusually long. Three fire department resignations in one meeting highlight ongoing staffing challenges due to competitive full-time fire service job market. Evan Cordray's father is noted as a Hamilton Township trustee. The township's first-ever officer-involved shooting was referenced in context of body camera value. Station 18 contract discussion was deferred pending prosecutor review of insurance papers.
title: BOT 5-14-24
